history.content:
<thought>
The goal is still at row 05 column E, three cells ahead and two cells to the right of my current position. The cell in front of me contains lava, so I cannot move forward. To reach the goal, I need to go right first and then up. Turning right will allow me to move right into the empty cell.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The goal is located at row 05 column B. The cell directly in front of me contains lava, so I cannot move forward. My current orientation is facing left, and moving left would put me into an unknown cell. Therefore, I need to turn right to move closer to the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The map shows that the only visible path is to the right, where there are no obstacles or lava cells blocking the way. My current position is at column F, row 6. Since I am facing right (towards column G), I can move forward to continue exploring the area to the right.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The goal is not currently visible in this view, but there are no lava cells directly in front of me. The path ahead is unknown, but I can move forward since the cell in front of me is marked as '.' (floor). Moving forward will help me explore the next part of the map.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The goal is not currently visible in this view, but the path forward seems clear until I encounter a wall. My current orientation is facing east, and the cell directly ahead is empty (`.`). Moving forward should take me closer to the goal without any immediate risks.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The current view does not provide clear information about the surroundings, but the agent is facing right at row 07, column B. The only known free cell is at row 07, column A, which is adjacent to the agent's current position. Moving forward seems like a logical step to explore the neighboring cells.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
My current position is at row 06, column B. The only visible floor cell in front of me is at row 07, column B. However, there is no clear path ahead as the cell directly in front of me is not visible. Given the lack of immediate threats and the need to explore, I will move forward to see if the path continues safely.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The map is mostly unknown, but I am located at column F, row 7. There are walls (#) surrounding me except to the left where there is a free floor (.). Since moving forward would keep me in an unknown area, and turning right or left does not offer immediate access to known clear space, I should explore the left side first by turning left.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The goal is located at row 05 column B, directly to the left of my current position. The cell in front of me contains lava, so I cannot move forward. To reach the goal, I need to turn left to face it.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The goal is located at row 05, column E. Currently, the cell in front of me (row 07, column 7) is a wall, so I cannot move forward. To the right of my current position, the cell at row 07, column 8 is empty. Moving right will bring me closer to the goal without encountering any lava cells.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>
